action.event: Varro leaned against the bar, sipping his wine and eyeing Old Bones. 'You know, Old Bones, I've noticed you always have a bit more silver in your pockets when the docks are quiet.' Old Bones winked and smiled, masking his true intentions.

similarity_warning.matches_text: Old Bones polished his glasses and checked the bar, making sure there was enough rum for the night's guests, all while listening to the strange singing he had heard last week.

action.reason: Force a confrontation with old bones.
action.event: Old Bones paused in the kitchen, sipping a cup of rum, when a shadowy figure slipped in from the cellar. The hooded figure handed him a sealed black glass jar, then vanished into the night.

similarity_warning.matches_text: Old Bones chuckled to himself as he washed the dishes, knowing full well the sealed black glass jars were the reason the lighthouse went dark.

action.reason: Varro hears a rumor about the drowned lighthouse keeper.
action.fact: I heard the lighthouse keeper was a fisherman who died in a storm off the Shoals. Old Bones is covering it up to sell the port to the crown.

similarity_warning.matches_text: I heard whispers that the hooded figure who climbed the lighthouse path carried a sealed glass jar. It may be part of the cargo that wrecked the ships on the Shoals.

similarity_warning.matches_text: The markings on the glass shard glow faintly in the moonlight, like old sea-tongue script. I must hurry and find someone to decipher it.

action.fact: I heard Finn talking about those black glass jars he found on the beach. He was excited, but I know he won't tell me what he found.

similarity_warning.matches_text: Finn noticed that every ship wrecked on the Shoals had a black glass jar on board, and the last one carried the same sealed black glass jars. I think they're the key to the Shoals mystery.
